hero.title: Networking
hero.subtitle: AtonixDev delivers isolated, scalable, developer-ready networking built for enterprise workloads.

architecture.title: Networking Architecture
architecture.description: AtonixDev networking is built on isolated virtual private networks, layered routing policies, and controlled ingress and egress paths — designed for enterprise and government-grade workloads.

subsections.title: Virtual Networks (VPCs)
subsections.body: Each tenant environment operates inside a dedicated Virtual Private Cloud. Network traffic is fully isolated between tenants with no shared routing paths. Environment segmentation — development, staging, and production — is enforced at the VPC boundary.
points: Dedicated CIDR blocks per tenant
No cross-tenant routing by default
Dev / Stage / Prod isolated per subnet group
Private DNS zones per environment

subsections.title: Subnets & Routing
subsections.body: Workloads are deployed across public and private subnets with strict routing table policies. Internal DNS resolves service endpoints without public exposure. All routing changes go through peer review before apply.
points: Public subnets for load balancers and API gateways
Private subnets for compute and data tiers
Route table policies enforced per subnet
Internal DNS with split-horizon configuration

subsections.title: Ingress & Egress
subsections.body: All inbound traffic passes through managed load balancers and API gateways with TLS termination. Egress is routed through NAT gateways with IP allowlists. Web Application Firewall rules protect public endpoints.
points: Layer 7 load balancers with TLS termination
API gateways for all public-facing services
NAT gateways with static egress IPs
WAF with OWASP ruleset and custom policies

subsections.title: Service Mesh
subsections.body: Internal service-to-service communication is secured with mutual TLS enforced by the service mesh layer. Automatic retry, circuit breaking, and traffic policies are defined per service without code changes.
points: mTLS for all internal service calls
Service discovery via internal DNS and registry
Traffic policies: retries, timeouts, circuit breakers
Distributed tracing and mesh-level observability

devPractices.title: Developer Networking Practices
devPractices.description: AtonixDev developers follow strict engineering disciplines when building and modifying networking infrastructure.

cards.title: Infrastructure as Code
cards.desc: All network configuration — VPCs, subnets, firewall rules, routing tables — is defined in version-controlled Terraform and Ansible. No manual changes are applied to production.

cards.title: Peer Review Gate
cards.desc: Every networking change must pass a formal peer review process before merge. Security and network engineers review all IaC pull requests targeting network resources.

cards.title: Automated Testing
cards.desc: Network changes are validated with automated connectivity tests, policy compliance checks, and regression suites before they enter any deployment pipeline stage.

cards.title: Deployment Pipelines
cards.desc: Network changes are promoted through dev → stage → production via CI/CD pipelines with approval gates. No direct production changes are allowed outside the pipeline.

cards.title: Monitoring & Alerting
cards.desc: All network flows, latency metrics, and security events are captured and monitored 24/7. On-call alerts fire on anomalies, policy violations, or connectivity degradation.

useCases.title: Networking Use Cases
useCases.description: Real-world scenarios demonstrating how AtonixDev networking capabilities deliver value in production environments.

scenarios.title: Multi-Environment Isolation
scenarios.desc: A customer's development, staging, and production environments are fully isolated within separate VPCs. Cross-environment traffic is blocked by default, with controlled peering available for approved data pipelines only.

scenarios.title: Hybrid Connectivity
scenarios.desc: Enterprise customers connect on-premises data centres to AtonixDev private networks via encrypted VPN tunnels or dedicated private interconnects, extending corporate networks without public exposure.

scenarios.title: Secure Public API Exposure
scenarios.desc: Public APIs are exposed through the API gateway tier, protected by WAF and rate limiting. Backend services remain in private subnets with no direct public IP assignments.

scenarios.title: Internal Microservice Communication
scenarios.desc: Services within the same environment communicate over private subnets using internal DNS names. mTLS enforces authentication and encryption for every service call without developer configuration.

compliance.title: Networking Standards & Compliance
compliance.description: AtonixDev networking design is aligned to enterprise and government security standards across zero-trust principles, encryption, and operational governance.

items.title: Zero-Trust Networking
points: No implicit trust based on network location
Every connection is authenticated and authorised
Least-privilege network access enforced at the route level
Continuous verification rather than perimeter-based trust

items.title: Encryption in Transit
points: TLS 1.2 minimum; TLS 1.3 enforced for internal services
mTLS for all east-west service-to-service traffic
Encrypted VPN tunnels for all hybrid connectivity
Certificate lifecycle managed by internal PKI

items.title: Segmentation Policies
points: Tenant isolation enforced at VPC and security group layer
Environment boundaries enforce strict traffic policies
Firewall rules reviewed quarterly and on every change
Network access logged and auditable

items.title: Logging & Monitoring
points: All network flows logged with source, destination, and protocol
Security event logs forwarded to SIEM
Real-time anomaly detection on traffic patterns
Retention aligned to compliance requirements — 90 days minimum

cta.title: Explore Networking Resources
cta.description: Access detailed documentation, API references, and direct support for AtonixDev networking capabilities.
